steps.title: Identify what "quality" refers to
steps.content: The passage describes Gatsby balancing on the dashboard with a "resourcefulness of movement that is so peculiarly American." The very next sentence then says "This quality was continually breaking through his punctilious manner." The word "This" points directly back to what was just described — so "quality" names the trait of resourceful, distinctly American movement.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Each option is a different sense of the word "quality." Check each against the passage:
  • Choice A — Standard: Standard refers to a level or degree of excellence — the passage is not rating how good Gatsby is at something, but naming a trait he has. ✗
  • Choice B — Prestige: Prestige means high status or reputation — the passage describes a physical manner of movement, not social standing. ✗
  • Choice C — Characteristic: A characteristic is a distinctive trait or feature — exactly what "resourcefulness of movement" is. ✓
  • Choice D — Accomplishment: Accomplishment means an achievement or feat — the passage describes an ongoing manner, not something Gatsby completed. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
